<input data-gherkin-criteria="
How to test a heading

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a heading

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the arrow key to browse to a heading I SEE the heading comes into view
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE nothing happens because headings must NOT be focusable

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to browse to a heading
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
  - I HEAR it identifies itself as a heading
  - I HEAR (if heading levels are used) it is logically structured
- THEN when I the tab key I HEAR nothing happens because headings must NOT be focusable

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/heading/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a heading

1. Test keyboard actions
- Arrow keys: browses to headings

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: the screenreader reads the heading (and if used, its level)

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it identifies itself as a heading
- Group: (if heading levels are used) it is logically structured

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/heading/" id="content-accessible-android-heading-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a decorative image

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a decorative image

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the arrow key to browse to a decorative image I SEE it ignores/skips the image completely
- THEN when I use the ctrl + tab key I SEE nothing happens because decorative images must NOT be focusable

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to browse to an image
  - I HEAR it ignores/skips the image completely

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/image-decorative/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a decorative image

1. Test keyboard actions
- Arrow keys: ignores/skips the image completely

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: ignores/skips the image completely

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: it ignores/skips the image completely

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/image-decorative/" id="content-accessible-android-image-decorative-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a floating action button

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a floating action button

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the action occurs

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on a button
  - I HEAR Its purpose is clear
  - I HEAR It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
  - I HEAR Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
  - I HEAR It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I doubletap with the button in focus I HEAR the action occurs

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-floating/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a floating action button

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: Activates the button

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear
- Role: It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
- Group: Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-floating/" id="actions-accessible-android-button-floating-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test an icon button

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with an icon button

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the action occurs

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on a button
  - I HEAR Its purpose is clear
  - I HEAR It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
  - I HEAR When applicable, a tooltip is available to help describe the icon button's purpose
  - I HEAR It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I doubletap with the button in focus I HEAR the action occurs

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-icon/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test an icon button

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: Activates the button

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear
- Role: It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
- Group: When applicable, a tooltip is available to help describe the icon button's purpose
- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-icon/" id="actions-accessible-android-button-icon-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a menu

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a menu

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a menu I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I SEE the menu opens
- THEN when I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to an option I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I SEE the option is selected
- OR when I use escape key I SEE the menu closes, focus returns to meaningful location

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on a menu or option
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
  - I HEAR it identifies itself as a button
  - I HEAR any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe, icons are ignored
  - I HEAR it expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I doubletap with the button or option in focus I HEAR the action occurs

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/menu/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a menu

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the menu and options
- Up/down arrows: opens closed menus, for open menus moves focus to the next item
- Left/right arrows: opens or closes a submenu
- Letters: focus moves to the next option starting with letter 
- Spacebar or enter: opens the menu or selects options
- Escape: closes menu, focus returns to meaningful location

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the menu or options, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: opens the menu or selects options

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe, icons are ignored
- State: it expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/menu/" id="actions-accessible-android-menu-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a segmented button

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a segmented button

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key to move focus to a segmented button I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the state changes

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on a button
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
  - I HEAR if single select it identifies as radio button
  - I HEAR if multiple select it identifies as checkbox
  - I HEAR its visible label (if any) is grouped or associated with the button in a single swipe
  - I HEAR it expresses its state (selected/disabled)
- THEN when I doubletap with the button in focus I HEAR the state changes

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see this element is exempt from text resizing requirements

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/segmented-button/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a segmented button

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: selects the button
      

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the buttons, expresses its state
- Doubletap: selects the button

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role single select: if single select it identifies as radio button
- Role multiple select: if multiple select it identifies as checkbox
- Group: its visible label (if any) is grouped or associated with the button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected/disabled)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: this element is exempt from text resizing requirements

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/segmented-button/" id="actions-accessible-android-segmented-button-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a badge

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a badge

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control with a badge I SEE focus is visually indicated on the entire control NOT the badge

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to browse to a control with a badge
  - I HEAR the name of the control, then badge content (Ex: Home, 1 new notification)

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/badge/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a badge

1. Test keyboard actions
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the control with a badge (like normal)
- Space or enter: activates any controls (like normal)

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the control with a badge (like normal)
- Doubletap: activates any controls (like normal)

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the name of the control, then badge content (Ex: Home, 1 new notification)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/badge/" id="communication-accessible-android-badge-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a progress indicator

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a progress indicator

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the arrow key to browse to a progress indicator I SEE the progress indicator comes into view

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to browse to a progress indicator
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and explains what items are waiting
  - I HEAR it identifies itself as a progress bar

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/progress-indicator/" data-condensed-criteria="

1. Test keyboard actions
- Arrow keys: browses to progress indicator

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: the screenreader reads the progress indicator

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and explains what items are waiting
- Role: it identifies itself as a progress bar

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

<input data-gherkin-criteria="
How to test a snackbar

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a snackbar

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use an action that opens a snackbar I SEE the snackbar text
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I SEE the snackbar can be browsed
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE focus moves to interactive controls in the snackbar

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I use an action that opens a snackbar
  - I HEAR the snackbar text is announced on appearing
  - I HEAR when dismissed manually, focus returns to meaningful next step
- THEN when I swipe I HEAR focus moves to the snackbar at the end of the screen
- THEN when I swipe within a snackbar I HEAR focus moves to interactive controls in the snackbar

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/snackbar/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a snackbar

1. Test keyboard actions
- Triggering action: focus does not move to the snackbar
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the snackbar at the end of the screen
- Space or enter: activates any controls in the snackbar

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the snackbar at the end of the screen
- Doubletap: activates any controls in the snackbar

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the snackbar text is announced on appearing
- Group: when dismissed manually, focus returns to meaningful next step

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/snackbar/" id="communication-accessible-android-snackbar-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a tooltip (rich)

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a tooltip (rich)

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control with a rich tooltip I SEE focus is visually indicated on the control
- THEN when I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control inside a rich tooltip I SEE focus is visually indicated on the control

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to browse to a control with a rich tooltip
  - I HEAR the content of the rich tooltip is announced after the control
  - I HEAR the control identifies its role (ex: Button)

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/tooltip-rich/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a tooltip (rich)

1. Test keyboard actions
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the control, causing the rich tooltip to display
- Tab or arrow keys: moves focus any controls in the rich tooltip
- Space or enter: activates any controls

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to a control causing the rich tooltip to be announced, moves focus through the  tooltip
- Doubletap: activates any controls

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the content of the rich tooltip is announced after the control
- Role: the control identifies its role (ex: Button)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/tooltip-rich/" id="communication-accessible-android-tooltip-rich-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

- Space or enter: activates any controls

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures

- Doubletap: activates any controls

- Name: the name of the control, matching the plain tooltip (but not both)

How to test a card

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a card

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to any nested control I SEE focus is strongly visually indicated
- THEN when I use the spacebar or enter to activate the control I SEE the action occurs

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to move focus to any nested control
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
  - I HEAR if non-actionable, any nested control expresses its role
  - I HEAR if directly actionable, it identifies as button or link
  - I HEAR any nested control expresses its state
- THEN when I doubletap with the control in focus I HEAR the control can be accessed
- OR when I when I swipe to move focus to any nested heading I HEAR the heading text and role of heading is announced

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/card/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a card

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to any control inside the card box
- Spacebar or enter: the control is selected

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to any element inside the cardbox
- Doubletap: intended actions occur

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role actionable: if non-actionable, any nested control expresses its role
- Role non-actionable: if directly actionable, it identifies as button or link
- State: any nested control expresses its state

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/card/" id="containment-accessible-android-card-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

<input data-gherkin-criteria="
How to test a carousel

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a carousel

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a slide I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the slide I SEE the action occurs

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on a slide or control
  - I HEAR the purpose of the carousel is clear
  - I HEAR it identifies as container
  - I HEAR the purpose of the slide is clear
  - I HEAR it identifies as list item, index (ex: 3 of 5)
- THEN when I doubletap with the slide or control in focus I HEAR the focused carousel item activates

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/carousel/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a carousel

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab or arrow keys: moves to the previous or next carousel item
- Spacebar or enter: activates the focused carousel item
 

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the next slide or controls
- Single point control: focus moves to the next slide (ex: Next button)
- Doubletap: activates the slides or controls

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Carousel name: the purpose of the carousel is clear
- Carousel role: it identifies as container
- Carousel slide: the purpose of the slide is clear
- Slide role: it identifies as list item, index (ex: 3 of 5)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/carousel/" id="containment-accessible-android-carousel-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

<input data-gherkin-criteria="
How to test a full screen dialog

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a full screen dialog

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to the launch button AND THEN use spacebar to activate the button I SEE the dialog opens
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I SEE content in the dialog is browsed in meaningful order and does not leave the dialog
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE focus moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- OR when I use the tab key to move focus to the dismiss/close button AND THEN use the spacebar to activate the dismiss/close button I SEE focus returns to the launch button

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus to the launch button
  - I HEAR the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
  - I HEAR it identifies itself as a dialog
  - I HEAR when closed, focus returns to the launch button
  - I HEAR when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
- THEN when I doubletap with the button in focus I HEAR the dialog opens
- THEN when I swipe within the dialog I HEAR focus stays trapped in the dialog
- THEN when I swipe to move focus to the dismiss/close button AND THEN double tap on the close button I HEAR focus returns to the launch button

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/dialog-full-screen/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a full screen dialog

1. Test keyboard actions
- Launch button: focus visibly moves to the open dialog itself
- Arrow keys: content within the dialog is browsed in meaningful order
- Tab: focus visibly moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- Escape: if dismissible, the dialog closes and returns focus to the button that launched it
- Space or enter: any buttons are activated

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within the dialog and doesn't enter the rest of the page
- Doubletap: activates controls

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- Role: it identifies itself as a dialog
- Group: when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- State: when open, content behind the dialog remains inert

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/dialog-full-screen/" id="containment-accessible-android-dialog-full-screen-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

How to test a date picker

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a date picker

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key to move focus to an individual control (text input, button, date) I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the selection is made

2 - Mobile screenreader
- WHEN I use Talkback screenreader AND
  - I swipe to focus on an individual control (text input, button, date)
  - I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches or extends visible label
  - I HEAR each item identifies its role (combo box for text input, button, full date for selection ex: "17" = "Monday, August 17")
  - I HEAR each component is a separately focusable item
  - I HEAR each item can expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I doubletap with the control in focus I HEAR the selection is made

3 - Device settings
- WHEN I use text resize THEN I see text can resize up to 200% without losing information

How to test a date picker

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to a date
- Spacebar or enter: activates the control
- Page up/down: move to the same date on next/previous month
- Home/end: move to the first day of the 
- Shift + page up/down: moves to the same date in the next/previous year
- Shift + m: moves to the month list dropdown
- Shift + y: moves to the year list dropdown

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the control

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches or extends visible label
- Role: each item identifies its role (combo box for text input, button, full date for selection ex: "17" = "Monday, August 17")
- Group: each component is a separately focusable item
- State: each item can expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/date-picker/" id="selection-accessible-android-date-picker-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a radio button

1 - Keyboard 
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a radio button I SEE focus is visually indicated

  - I HEAR it identifies itself as a radio button

How to test a radio button

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the checked radio button in the group. If a radio button is not checked, focus moves to the first radio button in the group
- Spacebar: if the radio button with focus is not selected, changes the state to selected
- Arrow-keys: moves focus to and selects the previous or next radio button in the group

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: if the radio button with focus is not selected, changes the state to selected. Otherwise, does nothing.

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a radio button
- Group: any visible label is read with the radio button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected, unselected, disabled)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/radio/" id="selection-accessible-android-radio-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/toggle-switch/" data-condensed-criteria="
How to test a toggle switch

1. Test keyboard actions
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the switch
- Spacebar or enter: switch toggles between states

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the switch, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: switch toggles between states

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a switch
- Group: any visible label is read with the switch in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (on/off, disabled)

4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/toggle-switch/" id="selection-accessible-android-toggle-switch-" class="criteria-checkbox" type="checkbox">

label_name_visible.0: Accessible name must match or contain the visible label text
label_name_visible.Fail_1: Accessible name does not match or contain the visible label text

form_submit_button_exists.0: A <form> element should have a submit button or an image button
form_submit_button_exists.Potential_1: Verify the <form> element has a submit button or an image button

input_checkboxes_grouped.0: Related sets of radio buttons or checkboxes should be programmatically grouped
input_checkboxes_grouped.potential_unnamedcheckbox: Verify that this un-named, ungrouped checkbox input is not related to other checkboxes

input_label_visible.0: An input element must have an associated visible label
input_label_visible.potential_no_label: The input element does not have an associated visible label

aria_keyboard_handler_exists.0: Interactive WAI_ARIA UI components must provide keyboard access
aria_keyboard_handler_exists.potential_no_keyboard_access: Verify the <{0}> element with "{1}" role has keyboard access
